label: Example dermal scenario-package probability bounds

supportPoints.note: Lower coupled-use package with reduced amount and frequency.

assumptions.name: use_amount_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product amount per event was supplied directly in grams.

assumptions.name: product_mass_g_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Mass-based use amount already represents product mass per event.

assumptions.name: concentration_fraction
assumptions.rationale: Chemical fraction in the product was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: use_events_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Use frequency was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: chemical_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product mass per event multiplied by concentration fraction.

assumptions.name: body_weight_kg

source.title: Benchmark population defaults mapped to EPA exposure-factor source families

assumptions.rationale: Resolved from population defaults for 'adult'.

assumptions.name: retention_factor

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP curated screening route-semantics defaults

assumptions.rationale: Retention factor defaulted from retention_type='leave_on'.

assumptions.name: transfer_efficiency

source.title: RIVM Cosmetics Fact Sheet hand-cream direct-application defaults

assumptions.rationale: Transfer efficiency defaulted from application_method='hand_application' and product_category='personal_care'.

assumptions.name: external_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Dermal external mass = chemical mass per event x events/day x retention x transfer efficiency.

assumptions.name: exposed_surface_area_cm2

source.title: Benchmark population defaults mapped to EPA exposure-factor source families

assumptions.rationale: Resolved from population defaults for 'adult'.

assumptions.name: product_mass_loading_mg_per_cm2_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product mass loading = product mass per event / exposed surface area.

assumptions.name: chemical_loading_mg_per_cm2_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Chemical loading = chemical mass per event / exposed surface area.

assumptions.name: normalized_external_dose_mg_per_kg_day
assumptions.rationale: Normalized external dose = external mass per day / body weight.

notes: Deterministic-first exposure engine.
All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

quality_flags.message: Population default 'body_weight_kg' uses region='EU' override (80.0 kg). Global default would differ.
Population default 'exposed_surface_area_cm2' uses region='EU' override (5700.0 cm2). Global default would differ.

The defaulted exposed_surface_area_cm2 (5700 cm² for adult, region='EU') is a hands/forearms screening anchor, not total body surface area. For whole-body products (e.g., body lotion), override this value.

tier_semantics.tier_rationale: Route-specific external exposure is produced with deterministic screening equations and may rely on default factor packs.
required_caveats: Interpret the result as an external dermal load at the skin boundary; it does not include dermal absorption.
forbidden_interpretations: Do not interpret this screening result as absorbed dermal dose, systemic exposure, or a final risk conclusion.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Tier A deterministic screening boundary remains in force
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The current result is governed as tier_a and tier_0; it is auditable but not probabilistic.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use named scenario envelopes for Tier B refinement before attempting probabilistic propagation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Known dependencies are preserved qualitatively, not numerically
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The output carries known dependence structures, but no joint sampling or correlation model is applied in Tier A.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use scenario packaging for Tier B and reserve probabilistic propagation for later tiers with explicit joint models.

dependencyMetadata.title: Use amount and frequency should be treated as a behavioral package
dependencyMetadata.note: Tier A/B keeps use amount and use frequency in named scenario packages instead of sampling them independently.

notes: Deterministic verification and benchmark regression are available for this domain.
External validation remains a documented future capability rather than an active gate.
Heuristic assumption names are emitted explicitly so evidence gaps stay attached to the scenario rather than hidden in source packs.
Probabilistic outputs stay disabled until dependencies and validation evidence mature.

interpretation_notes: Deterministic dermal screening scenario using explicit retention and transfer modifiers.

supportPoints.note: Reference coupled-use package aligned to the baseline screening archetype.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Scenario-package probability bounds preserve coupled drivers
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Profile `adult_leave_on_hand_cream_use_intensity_package` applies cumulative probability bounds to packaged `use_intensity` states that preserve coupled drivers.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat these outputs as bounded package frequencies, not as a full joint population exposure model.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Scenario-package probability limitations remain in force
uncertaintyRegister.summary: This package varies multiple use-intensity drivers together but still represents only three packaged support points.; The profile is not a validated consumer frequency distribution.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Keep the package profile tied to its published archetype set and avoid extrapolating it to unmanaged contexts.

dependencyMetadata.title: Adult leave-on hand cream use-intensity package packaged dependency bundle

interpretation_notes: This output applies probability bounds to packaged scenario states that preserve coupled drivers.
It is not a joint Monte Carlo simulation or a validated population exposure distribution.
Profile limitation: This package varies multiple use-intensity drivers together but still represents only three packaged support points.
Profile limitation: The profile is not a validated consumer frequency distribution.
